subject: Project Management
title: Why Waterfall Felt Right
description: A fast breakdown of Why Waterfall Felt Right for builders.

hook.text: Plan, execute, deliver. It's how we build houses, bridges, and spaceships. Why wouldn't it work for software?

buildup.text: Waterfall appeals to our love of order: clear phases, defined handoffs, measurable milestones.

discovery.text: Managers loved it because it promised predictability: tell me the cost and the date, and I'll approve it.

twist.text: The promise of predictability is seductive. But software requirements change — and waterfall assumes they won't.

climax.text: Waterfall felt right because it matched how humans think: linearly. Reality, unfortunately, is not linear.

punchline.text: We love straight lines. Projects don't follow them.

quiz.question: Why did waterfall appeal to managers?
options: It promised predictability with clear phases and defined milestones
It was cheaper than all alternatives
Developers preferred it over all other methods
